mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with declining FEDFUNDS, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0%, labor market slack suppresses corporate earnings growth expectations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of months where UNRATE > 7.0, or signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns (win rate < 50%), or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicates capacity constraints and rising input costs, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth predicts positive 5-day returns in late-cycle regimes, or the signal reverses direction (Rank IC > 0.05 positive) during capacity-constrained periods.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly inflation readings above 2.5% trigger expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL > 2.5 is followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls reflect strengthening economic activity and higher corporate revenue projections, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 5-day forward returns, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.01 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly real sales indicates weakening consumer momentum relative to aggregate output, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS spread shows no predictive power for 20-day returns (Rank IC < 0.03), or positive spread predicts positive returns instead.

mechanism_narrative: Synchronized declines in industrial production and employment during a recessionary phase signal severe demand destruction, but equity markets price in recovery expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint declines in INDPRO and PAYEMS predict negative 60-day returns in >60% of recessionary months, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant.
